title: synsepalum muelleri
intro: Synsepalum muelleri, also known as the African cherry or one-leafed calophyllum, is a plant species native to West Africa, characterized by its single leaf that can grow up to 30 cm in length. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate saliva production due to its unique compound, synsepelin, which temporarily makes food taste extremely sour. This plant has been found to have potential health benefits, such as aiding in digestion and potentially reducing the risk of certain diseases. The herbal preparations of Synsepalum muelleri include infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which are used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
